disclaimer_text: Paid for by Washingtonians for Bryant (R)
body:
| | |

---
| | Friend

Over a year and a half ago, I announced my candidacy to be your next governor. I said then I wanted to build a state where every kid, regardless of where they grow up, receives an education that gives them an equal chance to get ahead. I wanted to focus on getting our traffic moving, generating middle class jobs, helping our homeless, restoring salmon runs and making government better, not bigger.

Over these last eighteen months, you and thousands of others have joined me in this campaign. Republicans, Independents, and Democrats have united to help us win. That outpouring of support is incredibly humbling.

Tonight, Barbara and I and our entire family will watch the results of your hard work. All of us, my mom and mother-in-law who handed out thousands of cards that said “My Son for Governor,” my dad, my brother and sisters, my nieces and nephews, my in-laws who marched in parades, made phone calls, canvassed, and waved signs will together watch the votes come in. Their support throughout this campaign has been inspirational.

To all of you who donated, who attended an event, who shared a Facebook post, who forwarded an email, who made a phone call or knocked on a door, to everyone who put a sign in their yard, who came to our kickoff, and most of all, to everyone who cast a vote, I am humbled and grateful.

Barbara and I thank you.

Onward,

**Bill Bryant**

name: Deborah Ross
subject: An ending and a thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Deborah Ross for Senate Deborah Ross for Senate
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| The results are in. It's not the ending we hoped for, but I have zero regrets.

It was the highest honor of my lifetime to run for North Carolina's United States Senator and garner the support of millions of citizens.

We defied a lot of expectations to get this far, and I know I would not be here tonight without your support. For more than a year, you generously gave your time, your signatures, your dollars, and your unwavering belief in our future.

**To my supporters, my campaign team, my husband Steve and my parents, thank you from the bottom of my heart. Even though we didn 't win, what we did together was special.**

I know this election season was more contentious than most, and I know we have a lot of healing to do to bring people together. But if this election has shown me one thing, it's that you can stare down the politics of hate and fear by meeting people in their communities and showing you care.

Over the course of this campaign I've seen the very best of North Carolina. Our history. Our beauty. Our people.

While this campaign didn't end the way we hoped, I'm so inspired by the people I've met who I know will keep working each day for a better life for our families and communities.

**You have made this truly one of the best experiences of my life. Thank you so much for all that you did for our race.**

Very truly yours,

Deborah |

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hey there,
We always knew this race was going to be close. Right now, we're feeling good, but it's simply too close to call.
At this moment, we're tracking a small lead, but there are more votes to count in towns throughout New Hampshire.
The extraordinary work of volunteers and supporters across the state has fueled this campaign -- knocking on doors, calling voters, and giving what you can. I want to thank each and every one of you for your tireless efforts to move New Hampshire and America forward.
For now, get some rest tonight and hopefully we'll have good news to report in the morning.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
America won last night. |
[](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/ecfba1LcBAEI0xCExWgaCBEaDRMN6aaaaECH9BMCGXHya?p=7@1~1B3&j=Yurngqdx1A_brVng.hjr&g=g&s=&w=w6vCw~5ymzhu51dsnDYooDdscDekXDoDoDck5j56&oDo)

Friend,

They said we could never do it.

But last night you showed the world that America will once again be a country of, for, and by the PEOPLE.

You fought like a winner, you defied all odds, and history will forever remember the role you played in taking our country back.

I never could've done it without you, Friend.
[](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/ecfba1LcBAEI0xCExWgaCBEaDRMN6aaaaECH9BMCGXHya?p=7@1~1B3&j=Yurngqdx1A_brVng.hjr&g=i&s=&w=w6vCw~5ymzhu51dsnDYooDdscDekXDoDoDck5j56&oDo)

Last night we learned that America is still a beacon of hope where the impossible is possible.

For far too long, we've heard Washington politicians give the excuse that "it can't be done." They say we can't balance the budget, we can't stop corruption, we can't control the border, we can't bring jobs back to our country.

I REFUSE to accept that it can't be done. This is the country that declared its independence, won two world wars, and landed a man on the moon. This is America. We can and we WILL get it done.
Now it's time to start uniting our country and binding the wounds of our divided nation. I promise to be a president for ALL Americans. I will work for you. I will fight for you. And I will win for you.

You will soon remember what it's like to win as an American.

Thank you and God bless you,

Donald J. Trump

name: Katie McGinty
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate |
body:
Friends —

Thank you.

This isn’t the note I’d hoped to write today, but I want you to know how grateful I am. You turned out millions of votes for fairness, equality, and progress. In the face of long odds, you created a spark of change and hope. I will never forget it.

The result was disappointing, but I am so proud and grateful for what we accomplished. We left Pennsylvania better. Every vote cast, door knocked, phone call made, and grassroots donation helped build the foundation of a better, more hopeful future. I have no doubt that this team will continue to shape Pennsylvania and our nation.

So thank you — from the bottom of my heart — for everything. Now we look to tomorrow, and keep fighting and working together for the commonwealth we love.

Thank you —

Katie McGinty

----------------------------------------------

Friend,

From the bottom of my heart, I want to thank you for your support of my candidacy for Senate. We ran an excellent campaign that I'm proud of. Unfortunately, we fell short of a victory yesterday.

I ran for Senate because I was alarmed at what I was seeing in Washington. Partisanship and gridlock have reached untenable extremes. I wanted to offer a more positive and cooperative vision for our country, in which we can have disagreements but never forget that we're all in this together.

Millions of Hoosiers agreed with that vision -- and I know that you will carry it forward. You'll continue to fight for fair wages, affordable college education, an end to trade policies that hurt our workers, and much more. It may be a long struggle, but I am certain that you will succeed.

Every day on the campaign trail, I was inspired by the Hoosiers I met. You are our state's greatest asset, and you will achieve remarkable things. I wish Senator-elect Young all the best in serving the people of Indiana.

Thank you for lending your tremendous support to my campaign over the last several months.

Best,

Evan

name: Rob Portman
subject: We couldn't have done it without you
body:
| Peter \--

We couldn’t have done it without you. With your help, we ran a grassroots and issues-based campaign, reaching out to every voter in Ohio.

Now I am excited to begin a second term getting things done for the people of Ohio in the United States Senate.

From the very beginning, we have been amazed by the outpouring of support we have received from all across Ohio. Jane and I have traveled in the campaign RV and in my pickup truck to every corner of our state and every one of Ohio's 88 counties. We went to college campuses, factories, farms, and union halls. We’ve marched in parades, toured festivals, and spoken to thousands of Ohioans.

We have built the best grassroots campaign Ohio has ever seen. With 12 offices across the state, our thousands of volunteers and interns contacted 6 million Ohio voters through our door-to-door and phone banking efforts. And we used that data to effectively communicate with voters like never before.

I am honored to be able to continue to represent Ohio in the United States Senate, and I look forward to continuing to fight to grow jobs, increase wages, and ensure that every Ohioan can live up to his or her God-given potential.

Throughout this entire journey, Jane and I have really appreciated your ideas, your support, and your prayers. Thank you again for being a part of our team, and I hope to see you soon.

All best,

Rob Portman [](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=after_election&n=1)[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=after_election&n=2)[

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: We've declared victory
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello --
As we've gotten more results overnight and this morning, and with 100% of the vote tallied, it's clear that we have maintained the lead and have won this race.
I am proud to write to you as the next United States Senator from New Hampshire.
New Hampshire and America have clearly been sent a message about the direction Donald Trump wants to take us -- and it will be my job in the U.S. Senate to make the best decisions possible for hard-working families by working with President-elect Trump when he's right and standing up to him when he's wrong.
As you all know, I entered public service as an advocate for people who experience disabilities. People whose voices are too often not heard. I believe we are stronger when we listen to all voices and all perspectives. We are stronger when we are working to ensure that every person has the opportunity to share in our nation's success.
And ensuring that we are expanding opportunity to all of our citizens will be my guiding principle in the United States Senate.
Together, we will work to heal the divisions this election has exposed and build a future where all Americans are included in our shared economic success.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

name: Patrick Murphy
subject: thank you, Florida
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Patrick Murphy | 4521 PGA Blvd #412, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418 | [http://murphyforflorida
body:
| |
---
| | []()
---
| Thank you so much. I'm so proud of the campaign we built together, and so grateful for the passion you put into this fight. We hoped for a different result, but the people of Florida have spoken, and I respect their choice. While Senator Rubio and I have our differences, it is time to come together. Floridians are counting on him to fight for them, and he has my support in that fight.

To the families of Florida's 18th district, it has been an honor to represent you. Thank you for believing in me, and more importantly believing in what we could accomplish together.

To my team, volunteers, and supporters across the state: thank you from the bottom of my heart. We worked for Florida together, and I wouldn't have it any other way.

It has been the honor of a lifetime to be your Democratic nominee. Together, we'll keep fighting for what's right for our state and our nation.

Thank you, again, for everything.

Sincerely,
Patrick |

name: Russ Feingold
subject: Thank you.
disclaimer_text: paid for by special interest groups
body:
We accomplished so much together. Thank you. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf46b6/1c469400/2b3e32b/35459f5/1408179113/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-en?utm_source=ema_2016-11-08_EN_ND&refcode=ema_2016-11-08_EN_ND&utm_campaign=EN&utm_medium=ema") Friend, When we first launched this campaign over a year ago, I made a simple promise: That I'd listen to you. So I hit the road every morning, traveling to each of the state's 72 counties to meet people where they are. We logged over 70,000 miles on our campaign van. I've lost count of the number of coffees and roundtables- but I'll always remember the conversations I had along the way and the Wisconsinites I met. What I've heard, time and time again, is that people are fed up with politicians who lecture instead of listen. They're tired of politicians that are bought and paid for by special interest groups. They're having a hard time making ends meet, and they just want a fair shot. _But not one person told me they wanted to stop fighting and just give up._ And we didn't. We ran a progressive campaign, focused on raising the minimum wage, fighting for paid family leave, protecting and expanding social security, and making college affordable -- all issues that will make it easier for hardworking families. At the beginning of this campaign, I said that if you work hard, you should be able to pay the bills, put a little away, and enjoy some time with your family. I really wanted to get this done. But something is happening in our country. I don't understand it completely. I don't think anybody does. But we as Americans have to do the best we can to deal with the pain in this country and get people to come together. **We've accomplished so much together. And even though we didn't come out on top last night, I'm proud of the work we did together.** Thank you for your support. Keep fighting. Sincerely, Russ Feingold

CONCORD – Today, New Hampshire Republican State Committee (NHGOP)
Chairman Jennifer Horn released the following statement on the
Republicans' historic victories in the New Hampshire State House
of Representatives, New Hampshire State Senate, Executive
Council, and the corner office:

“Last night was a very good night for New Hampshire Republicans
as we achieved historic victories all the way down the ballot and
retained our majorities in the Executive Council, State Senate,
and State House. Republicans throughout the state ran
results-oriented campaigns and offered solutions to some of our
state’s most pressing issues. We can all be proud of their
hard-fought efforts. We ran a complete coordinated effort and
executed the largest data-driven Republican ground operation in
our state's history.

“I want to congratulate Governor-elect Chris Sununu, our first
Republican in the corner office in 12 years. Under Governor-elect
Sununu’s leadership, he will work with state leaders to cut red
tape, boost the economy, create good-paying jobs right here in
New Hampshire, and put forth a serious plan to combat the opioid
epidemic. Governor-elect Sununu will change the failed Democrat
status quo that we have seen for far too long in Concord.

“For the first time in years, Republicans have control of the
corner office, both chambers of the Statehouse, and the Executive
Council. Today is truly a historic day in New Hampshire and under
Republican leadership, our state will grow to new limits.”

clean_body: NHGOP STATEMENT ON GUBERNATORIAL RACE AND SWEEPING DOWN BALLOT VICTORIES FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE:11/9/2016 PRESS CONTACT: Ross Berry, press@nhgop.org ( ) CONCORD – Today, New Hampshire Republican State Committee (NHGOP) Chairman Jennifer Horn released the following statement on the Republicans' historic victories in the New Hampshire State House of Representatives, New Hampshire State Senate, Executive Council, and the corner office: “Last night was a very good night for New Hampshire Republicans as we achieved historic victories all the way down the ballot and retained our majorities in the Executive Council, State Senate, and State House. Republicans throughout the state ran results-oriented campaigns and offered solutions to some of our state’s most pressing issues. We can all be proud of their hard-fought efforts. We ran a complete coordinated effort and executed the largest data-driven Republican ground operation in our state's history. “I want to congratulate Governor-elect Chris Sununu, our first Republican in the corner office in 12 years. Under Governor-elect Sununu’s leadership, he will work with state leaders to cut red tape, boost the economy, create good-paying jobs right here in New Hampshire, and put forth a serious plan to combat the opioid epidemic. Governor-elect Sununu will change the failed Democrat status quo that we have seen for far too long in Concord. “For the first time in years, Republicans have control of the corner office, both chambers of the Statehouse, and the Executive Council. Today is truly a historic day in New Hampshire and under Republican leadership, our state will grow to new limits.” Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.nh.gop ( ) footer image

name: =?utf-8?Q?Eric=20Greitens?=
subject: We Did It!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Peter,
We did it! We did it because of your incredible dedication and devotion, your heart and hard work.

Sheena and I thank all of you. Thank you for your faith, your prayers, your hard work, your courage, and your sacrifice. Thank you for having our back.

Around this state, thousands of you joined us in a grassroots campaign—a movement that hasn’t been seen in Missouri for generations. There were law enforcement officers and first responders who brought their friends and family out and made thousands of phone calls. High school students who woke up before the sun rose to put up campaign signs. And so many strong veterans around the state who stood beside us.

This campaign reminded us that great teams can be formed out of great struggle. All of us on this campaign knew how much was at stake. We knew how many people were counting on us. That mission gave us clarity and courage. That shared purpose gave us strength.

Our mission was—and is—to create a stronger Missouri, to take this state in a new and better direction. And we are going to work to make sure we bring more jobs, higher pay, safer streets, and better schools to our state.

What we earned yesterday is something sacred: the privilege to serve. As your Governor, I intend to be a servant-leader. As citizens, we all need to serve alongside and for each other. Now we must take all of this energy, all of this compassion, all of this courage—and use it to build a Missouri we can be proud to pass on to our kids and grandkids.

Sheena and I are honored, humbled, and inspired to be on this journey with you.

Almost three thousand years ago, the poet Homer wrote the story of an ancient warrior named Odysseus. His war was over, and Odysseus and his crew faced a difficult journey home. Thinking of the obstacles and the uncertainty ahead, they said: “We will endure it, keeping a stubborn spirit inside us. We have done much hard work on the waves and in the fighting. Now, let this adventure begin.”

Our adventure—our next mission—begins today.

Yours in service,

Eric

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

St. Louis, Missouri 63108

name: tom@dscc.org
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
This one hurts, Friend. I wish I could tell you today there was a Democratic majority in the Senate that could easily push back...
---
This one hurts, Friend.

I am so sorry that I cannot tell you there is a Democratic majority in the Senate. We all worked hard together to win a Democratic majority - and while we didn't make it, please know that I appreciate all you did to further the fight.

While many are stunned by the choice voters made, I do know this: We picked up two seats, and the new Democratic Senators you elected (including Tammy Duckworth, Kamala Harris, Maggie Hassan, Catherine Cortez Masto and Chris Van Hollen) will work non-stop to protect the values we hold dear, including President Obama's legacy.

We have accomplished such incredible progress over the last decade. We made it clear that everyone deserves access to health care, that women's rights will not be trampled, that our LGBT communities deserve equal rights, and so much more.

None of those fights could have been won without your tenacity, grit, and dedication.

And because of this I know we will not be knocked out. We will get back up and stand up for the America we love.

But today, we must take a moment to grieve. And then in the words of my late boss, Senator Edward M. Kennedy, _" the work goes on, the cause endures, the hope still lives, and the dream shall never die."_

Thank you for being our partner in this fight and for truly making this a grassroots community.

In gratitude,
Tom Lopach
